name: Albert Brooks
bio: Albert Brooks is an American comedian, actor, director, and screenwriter who led a generation of self-reflective comics on The Tonight Show in the early 1970s with an ironic, self-puncturing showbiz-insider persona. After two acclaimed comedy albums he made early short films for the first season of Saturday Night Live and moved into filmmaking, writing and directing comedies such as Real Life, Lost in America, and Defending Your Life. He earned an Academy Award nomination for Broadcast News (1987).

timeline.ev: Releases the mockumentary short The Famous Comedians School
A Star Is Bought earns a Grammy nomination; directs short films for SNLs first season
Acts in Martin Scorseses Taxi Driver
Directorial feature debut with Real Life
Writes, directs, and stars in Lost in America
Oscar nomination for Best Supporting Actor in Broadcast News

accolades: Grammy nomination for the comedy album A Star Is Bought (1975)
Created the SNL short-film format in its first season (1975)
Academy Award nomination, Best Supporting Actor, Broadcast News (1987)

ranking_notes: Matthew asked for a decent spread of old + recent. Got the 1979 SNL conceptual short (old) plus the 2015 SNL Backstage retrospective (recent). His existing bits should keep both bookends.
